docs.title: GOAP Planner Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the documentation for goapauto, a strictly-typed Goal-Oriented Action Planning system for Python.

docs.title: 📚 User Guide
docs.text:
Start here to learn how to build agents.

  • Getting Started: Build your first agent in 5 minutes.
  • Core Concepts: Understanding WorldState, Goals, and the Planning Loop.

docs.title: ⚙️ API Reference
docs.text:
Detailed reference for classes and functions.

  • WorldState: State management.
  • Goals: Defining objectives.
  • Actions: Defining behaviors and effects.
  • Planner: The A* planning engine.
  • Sensors & Arbitrators: Advanced sensing and goal selection.
  • Visualizer: Debugging tools.

docs.title: goapauto.models.actions.Action
docs.text:
Represents an atomic action the agent can take.

from goapauto.models.actions import Action, Increment

action = Action(
    name="chop_wood",
    preconditions={"has_axe": True},
    effects={"wood": Increment(1)},
    cost=1.0 # int or float (default: 1)
)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• is_applicable(state: Any) -> bool Returns True if the state meets all preconditions.

  • apply(state: Any) -> WorldState Returns a new WorldState with effects applied (immutable transition).

  • async_apply(state: Any) -> WorldState Coroutine version of apply for async contexts.

docs.title: goapauto.models.actions.Predicate
docs.text: Base class for conditions. - Equal(value): Checks equality. - GreaterThan(value): Checks state_val > value. - LessThan(value): Checks state_val < value.

docs.title: goapauto.models.actions.Effect
docs.text: Base class for state mutations. - Set(value): Sets attribute to value. - Increment(amount=1): Adds amount. - Decrement(amount=1): Subtracts amount.

docs.title: goapauto.models.goal.Goal
docs.text:
Represents a target state to achieve.

from goapauto.models.goal import Goal

goal = Goal(
    target_state={"has_wood": True},
    priority=1, # Lower number = higher priority (must be >= 1)
    name="Collect Wood"
)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• is_satisfied(world_state: Any) -> bool Checks if all conditions in target_state are met by world_state.

  • get_unsatisfied_conditions(world_state: Any) -> Dict Returns conditions that are not yet met in the current state.

docs.title: goapauto.models.sensors.SensorManager
docs.text:
Orchestrates multiple Sensor instances to update WorldState.

manager = SensorManager()
manager.add_sensor(MySensor())
manager.update_state(state)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• add_sensor(sensor: Sensor): Register a sensor.
  • update_state(state: WorldState) -> WorldState: Runs all sensors and updates the state.

docs.title: goapauto.models.sensors.Sensor (Abstract)
docs.text: Base class for sensors. Implement sense() -> Dict[str, Any].

docs.title: goapauto.models.goal_arbitrator.GoalArbitrator
docs.text:
Selects the highest priority satisfiable goal from a list.

arbitrator = GoalArbitrator(goals=[goal1, goal2])
selected_goal = arbitrator.select_goal(state)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• add_goal(goal: Goal): Add a goal to manage.
  • remove_goal(name: str): Remove a goal by its name.
  • select_goal(state: WorldState) -> Optional[Goal]: Returns best valid goal.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: The GoalArbitrator accepts a custom GoalSelectionStrategy in its constructor. The default is PriorityGoalStrategy (lowest priority number wins).

docs.title: goapauto.models.goap_planner.Planner
docs.text:
The A* search engine used to find a plan.

from goapauto.models.goap_planner import Planner

planner = Planner(actions_list=[...])
result = planner.generate_plan(start_state, goal)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• generate_plan(world_state, goal, max_depth=None, heuristic_fn=None) -> PlanResult Synchronous plan generation. Returns a PlanResult.

  • async_generate_plan(world_state, goal, max_depth=None, heuristic_fn=None) -> PlanResult Asynchronous version.

  • register_hook(event: str, callback: Callable) Register callbacks for events: on_node_expanded, on_plan_found, on_search_failed.

docs.title: Types
docs.text:
• PlanResult: NamedTuple (plan: Optional[List[str]], message: str).
  • HeuristicFn: Callable[[WorldState, Goal], float].

docs.title: goapauto.utils.visualizer.SearchTreeVisualizer
docs.text:
Hooks into the planner to visualize the search tree.

viz = SearchTreeVisualizer()
planner.register_hook("on_node_expanded", viz.on_node_expanded)
# ... plan ...
viz.export("tree.mmd")

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• export(filepath: str) -> None Saves the tree to a file. Supports .mmd (Mermaid).

  • to_mermaid() -> str Returns the raw Mermaid diagram string.

  • to_graphviz() -> str Returns the raw Graphviz DOT string.

  • clear() Resets the captured data.

docs.title: goapauto.models.worldstate.WorldState
docs.text:
A class representing the world state with attribute-style access. Inherits from pydantic.BaseModel.

from goapauto.models.worldstate import WorldState

# Initialization (Keyword-only arguments)
state = WorldState(has_wood=True, count=5)
# dictionary access
val = state['has_wood']
# attribute access
val = state.has_wood

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• update(other: Union[Dict, WorldState], **kwargs) -> None Updates the state with values from another state/dict or keyword arguments.

  • diff(other: WorldState) -> Dict[str, tuple[Any, Any]] Returns non-matching keys between self and other as (self_val, other_val).

  • copy(deep: bool = False) -> WorldState Returns a copy of the state.

  • get(key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any Safe accessor for state components.

  • to_dict() -> Dict[str, Any] Converts state to a standard dictionary.

docs.title: Core Concepts & Architecture
docs.text: goapauto implements a standard Goal-Oriented Action Planning architecture modularized for Python.

docs.title: 1. World Model (WorldState)
docs.text:
• Responsibility: Stores the current state of the environment and agent.
  • Design: Pydantic model with dynamic attribute access (state.key or state['key']).
  • Mutability: Methods like update() mutate the instance; copy() and apply() create new instances.

docs.title: 2. Action System
docs.text:
• Action: Atomic unit containing:
    • preconditions: Predicates (Equal, GreaterThan) that must match the world state.
    • effects: Transformations (Set, Increment) applied to the state.
    • cost: Floating point cost for A* pathfinding.
  • Planner: Uses A* search to find the lowest-cost sequence of actions connecting Start State -> Goal State.

docs.title: 3. Perception & Arbitration (The "Brain" Loop)
docs.text:
For continuous agents, the system operates in a loop:

  1. Sense: SensorManager aggregates data from Sensors to update WorldState.
  2. Think (Arbitrate): GoalArbitrator evaluates all Goals and selects the highest-priority one that is not yet satisfied.
  3. Plan: Planner generates a specific plan to satisfy the selected Goal.
  4. Act: The agent executes the plan (actions).

docs.title: Data Flow Diagram
docs.text:
graph TD
    Env[Environment] -->|Sense| Sensors
    Sensors -->|Update| State[WorldState]
    State -->|Input| Arbitrator
    Goals[Goal List] -->|Input| Arbitrator
    Arbitrator -->|Selected Goal| Planner
    Actions[Action Set] -->|Available Actions| Planner
    Planning[A* Search] -->|Plan| Execution
    Execution -->|Apply| Env

docs.title: Design Decisions
docs.text:
• Loose Coupling: Sensors and Arbitrators are optional. You can use the Planner purely as a pathfinding utility.
  • Strict Typing: All core models enforce types to catch configuration errors early.
  • Observability: Hooks and Visualization allow introspection into the planning process (White-box AI).

docs.title: User Guide: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide explains how to use goapauto to build intelligent agents.

docs.title: 1. Define the World State
docs.text:
The WorldState is the agent's memory or belief about the world. It is initialized with keyword arguments.

from goapauto.models.worldstate import WorldState

# Define initial state
state = WorldState(
    hungry=True,
    has_food=False,
    location="bedroom"
)

docs.title: 2. Define Actions
docs.text:
Actions transform the state. They have preconditions (requirements) and effects (changes).

from goapauto.models.actions import Action, Set

# Action: specific definition
eat_action = Action(
    name="eat_food",
    preconditions={"has_food": True},
    effects={
        "hungry": False, 
        "has_food": False
    },
    cost=1.0
)

# Action: using reusable Effects
move_to_kitchen = Action(
    name="goto_kitchen",
    preconditions={"location": "bedroom"},
    effects={"location": Set("kitchen")},
    cost=2.0
)

docs.title: 3. Define Goals
docs.text:
A Goal describes a target state the agent wants to reach.

from goapauto.models.goal import Goal

goal = Goal(
    target_state={"hungry": False},
    priority=1
)

docs.title: 4. Run the Planner
docs.text:
Feed the actions into the Planner and request a plan to the goal.

from goapauto.models.goap_planner import Planner

planner = Planner(actions_list=[
    # You can pass raw tuples or Action objects
    (eat_action.name, eat_action.preconditions, eat_action.effects, eat_action.cost),
    (move_to_kitchen.name, move_to_kitchen.preconditions, move_to_kitchen.effects, move_to_kitchen.cost)
])

result = planner.generate_plan(state, goal)

if result.plan:
    print("Plan found:", result.plan)
    # Output: ['goto_kitchen', 'eat_food']
else:
    print("No plan found:", result.message)

docs.title: 5. Execution (Advanced)
docs.text:
In a real game loop, you would Execute the first action in the plan, then re-evaluate the state or re-plan.

See examples/example1.py for a complete runnable bot.
